titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the J. Neilson Barry Collection of Western Maps
Barry (J. Neilson) Collection of Western Maps
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 14563393.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 06:27

did.unittitle: J. Neilson Barry collection of western maps

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Published articles and manuscript maps created by John Neilson Barry, circa 1912-1944. Articles have been handbound by Barry and include his manuscript and typescript annotations as well as his illustrated maps of Oregon, Washington, and Idaho. Materials pertain to the histories of Oregon and Washington with a focus on expeditions through the Pacific Northwest and along the Columbia River, specifically the Astor Expedition (1810-1813), Lewis and Clark Expedition (1804-1806), and John Colter's explorations (1807-1808).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of J. Neilson Barry, 1942-1943. Transferred from Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title assigned by J. Neilson Barry to bound works.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Neilson Barry (1870-1961) of Wilmington, North Carolina, served as an Episcopal minister in Spokane, Washington; New York, New York; Washington, District of Columbia; and Baker, Oregon, from 1895 to 1913. From 1913 to 1922, Barry served as a probation officer with the police court in Spokane. He retired in 1922 and moved to Portland, Oregon, where he conducted historical research, focusing on maps of the Pacific Northwest and showing interest in John Colter, a member of the Lewis and Clark Expedition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: J. Neilson Barry Collection of Western Maps. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Folders have been titled according to original titles given by J. Neilson Barry to individual maps and published articles. Supplied folder titles are bracketed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published articles and manuscript maps created by John Neilson Barry, circa 1912-1944. Articles have been handbound by Barry and include manuscript and typescript annotations as well as illustrated maps of Oregon, Washington, and Idaho. Materials pertain to the histories of Oregon and Washington with a focus on expeditions through the Pacific Northwest and along the Columbia River, specifically the Astor Expedition (1810-1813), Lewis and Clark Expedition (1804-1806), and John Colter's explorations (1807-1808). Correspondence in the collection is from Barry to Sterling Memorial Library staff, providing supplementary information to his bound works.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The J. Neilson Barry Collection of Western Maps is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: The 15 Astorians Who Became Permanent Settlers
The Exploration of the Columbia River
First Explorers of the Columbia River
The Site of the Granary Old Mission
To Show Point Vancouver and Crown Point
U. S. Grant at Vancouver, Wash.
[Irving, Washington. Astoria (New York, G. P. Putnam), undated copy annotated by J. Neilson Barry]
[Printed map of automobile roads in Oregon]
[Printed maps and pamphlets of Yellowstone National Park]
